items.reviewerNotes: Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source ROW statement matches q0002 (safe distance ~100m at 100+ km/h); options are Yes/No so map Right->A. Slight wording difference (100 vs above 100) reduces confidence.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Exact semantic match to q0471 (driver flees causing death → prison >7 years). Local correct option D matches master D.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows traffic police signalling; q0464 (stop and wait) matches. Local option D = 's'arrêter et attendre' corresponds to q0464 answer.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Direct ROW unique-keyword match: source says a driver does not need to yield to a pedestrian violating traffic rules, and q0978 has the same statement with Right as the master answer. Local Yes=A maps to Right; legal wording is still worth human QC.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks how to turn right; q0442's correct answer is to yield to oncoming left-turning vehicle. Local option A matches that meaning. Machine top candidate differed, so confidence reduced. Rejected top matcher: Machine top match (q0510) is about speed limits and is unrelated to this image/question.

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Rejected q0114: it is specifically about temporary stopping in snowy conditions and its distractor rows differ. Source asks the generic roadside-stopping light question; source answer C is hazard lights, so this should be a new localized question rather than a broad q0114 approval.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement that night observation and visibility shorten matches q0101 (Right). Local option A = Yes maps to Right.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Sign is triangular warning showing a hump/bump; q0782 means 'speed bump ahead'. Local options C and D both reference bumps; D ('ralentisseur de type dos-d'ane') most directly maps to 'speed bump'.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Road marking arrow points left/merge left; q0830 correct = 'left curve or merge left'. Local option A matches exactly.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows arrows for straight and right; q0746 correct = 'go straight or turn right'. Local option C corresponds to that meaning.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source ROW statement = 'You must not drive vehicles with safety risks' matches q0982 (same assertion). Source options: A Yes (True) maps to master correctRow Right.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image: circular red-bordered '40' = speed limit 40 km/h. q0752 correct master choice is 'maximum speed limit 40 km/h' (master D) which corresponds to the source option B (vitesse maximale limitée à 40 km/h).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Direct master search found q0279: driving after drinking is subject to a 12-point penalty. This is a closer ROW match than the top candidate q0312, which only discusses license detention after reaching 12 points. Local Yes=A maps to Right.

Codex high-risk keepUnresolved: Image shows a blank red-bordered circular prohibition sign but is visually ambiguous (no pictogram). Candidate matches are weak/mismatched. Cannot confidently map to an existing qid or to one of the provided answer texts (no-entry vs no-vehicles etc.).

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: Source is valid and has answer A=12 points, but no close master qid was found for driving a vehicle category that does not match the license class. q0981 and q0487 are different offences despite sharing the 12/6/3/2 option set.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Sign shows 'X008' rectangular route marker. q0882 correctOption = county/district road No. which maps to source option C (numéro de la voie du district).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows right-turn indicator arrow illuminated (right arrow green). q0622 in candidate list corresponds to 'right-turn signal flashes' — maps to source option B ('le clignotant de virage à droite s'allume'). Some candidate conflict exists (other qids suggest sidelights), so moderate risk.

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Predicate asks if left indicator lit obliges you to turn left — correct traffic-logic answer is 'No', but provided machine candidates focus on which indicator corresponds to the image (left vs right). Image and candidate set are ambiguous; do not auto-approve mapping to an existing qid.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows police hand signals; q0899 master answer is 'stop' which corresponds to source option B (s'arrêter). Visual/human semantics align with candidate.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks best place to stop by roadside; q0467 correct master text is 'stop in the parking area' which matches source option C ('l'intérieur d'une place de parking délimitée par le marquage au sol').
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source ROW statement (braking on muddy road causes skids) matches q0001 prompt and top candidate; 'Yes' = True corresponds to option A.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Triple red crossbuck indicates multiple railway tracks (multi-crossing). Top candidate q0837 means 'multi-crossing'; that meaning corresponds to source option D.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Actual image and image-color-tags match q0798: G15 entrance-ahead sign with 2 km and entry arrow. Local option B is highway entrance warning. Rejected top matcher q0366 as unrelated and q0799 because it is the beginning sign without the 2 km entrance-ahead wording.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a speed dial with odometer. q0599 = 'speed and mileage meter' matches; source option B is 'Indicateur de vitesse et kilomètre-compteur'.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0506 correct answer is 'criminal detention and a fine'; source option A 'détention et amende' corresponds to that meaning.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a turn-signal stalk; q0619 correct = 'turn signals'. Source option B = 'les clignotants' matches.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Actual source stripe orientation matches q0797 red/white obstacle marker for passing by the right side. Local option D is circulation a droite. This uses image orientation rather than the unrelated top matcher.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows '40' with diagonal strike indicating end/lift of 40 km/h limit. Candidate q0842 means '40km/hr speed limit ban is lifted' — source option C corresponds to that meaning.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: No U-turn sign clearly visible. q0202 states 'You can make an U-turn at this intersection.' correct = Wrong. Source option B = 'No' corresponds to Wrong.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Direct image and option-row match: source road marking is the same white 80 marking as q0744, and option A maps to minimum speed limit 80 km/h. The option rows align; approve q0744 with local key A.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a vehicle to the right with priority; source asks if you have priority (No). Candidate q0304 is ROW with correctRow=Wrong (matches 'No') and image parity.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Sign is a green highway direction sign with an arrow pointing left. q0824's meaning is 'highway left exit ahead' — that maps to the source's option D (sortie gauche).

Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Source asks if a right indicator lit obliges a right turn (expected answer: No). Top candidates relate to indicator lamps but prompts don't exactly match the logical rule; image shows left arrow lit. Not enough precise match to approve an existing qid.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Direct master search found q0676 with the same prompt: white broken-line area on the far left side. Option rows align exactly; local A is the multi-passenger special lane.
Codex medium-risk keepUnresolved: Source asks max speed on an 'autoroute' (highway). Top candidates disagree (city road 50km/h vs highway 70km/h). Visual is ambiguous; do not auto-approve a qid.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement 'It is illegal to make phone calls while driving' matches q0091 ('Using a mobile phone while driving is considered a violation'). Correct = Yes (A).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Near-match correction: q0177 is the closest prompt and option-row match for being overtaken by another vehicle. q0970 adds a following-vehicle condition not present in the French source. Local option B maps to reduce speed and keep right.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source states resting left shoulder on door or holding gear lever is a bad habit (Yes). q0065 has the same prompt and is True/Right — matches.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Actual sign and image-color-tags match q0686: blue car lane sign. Option-row comparison maps the master correct answer lane for motor vehicles to French option C, not the prior create-new key A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source statement 'cannot drive into lane where red X or red arrow is on' matches q0218 exactly. Correct = Yes (A).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Top candidate q0007 has virtually identical wording: avoid stopping in landslide/mudslide-prone mountain sections. French options map Yes=A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Top candidate q0009 matches exactly: if vehicle has seat belts driver should require passengers to fasten them. Source Yes=A.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Candidate q0132 directly addresses continuous braking on long downhill: correct option corresponds to French C (braking effect reduced due to rising brake temperature).
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0054 prompt matches: drive safely at prescribed/authorized speed. Source Yes=A.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Option-row search supports q0463: product qualification, keeping-distance label, danger reminder, and inspection label all map to the French options; master correct inspection label maps to local D (Controle Technique). Prompt wording is awkward, so keep as manual-QC risk.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0422 asks when driver not allowed to drive; correct master answer = 'driving license lost/destroyed'. In the localized option set that meaning corresponds to A (permis perdu ou endommagé).

Codex medium-risk createNewQuestion: No close candidate match found. Source is a valid road-accident rule (mark original position if you moved evidence while assisting); not represented in top candidates — create new question. Local correct option is Yes=A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0579 states ABS maximizes braking efficiency during sudden/hard braking; French option D 'freinage brutal' matches master correct answer.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0203 explicitly says traffic police can detain vehicles without mandatory traffic-accident insurance; source matches that meaning (Yes=A).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: q0205 matches the rule about a driver involved in a fatal accident who wasn't deprived of license must submit/renew within 30 days after the point-accumulation period; localized statement aligns (Yes=A).
